title: Valor Earned Not Stolen Act of 2025
short_title: Valor Earned Not Stolen Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill makes the punishment stronger for people who lie about winning military medals or awards to get money or other benefits. Right now, people who do this can go to prison for up to one year, but this bill would increase that to up to three years. The bill covers important military awards like the Medal of Honor and Purple Heart. It also tells the Attorney General to study how people fake having military awards to get benefits they don't deserve and report back to Congress about it.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Valor Earned Not Stolen Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Fraudulent Misrepresentation About Receipt of a Military Medal or Decoration
sections.text: This section makes the punishments stricter for people who lie about receiving military medals or decorations. First, if someone lies about getting military decorations or medals, they can now go to prison for up to three years instead of just one year. Second, if someone lies about receiving the Congressional Medal of Honor (the highest military award), they can now go to prison for up to three years instead of just one year. Third, if someone lies about receiving a combat badge (a special award given to soldiers who fought in battle), they can now go to prison for up to three years instead of just one year. The Attorney General (the top law enforcement officer in the country) must work with the Inspector General of the Department of Veterans Affairs and other government agencies to study this problem. Within ninety days after this law is passed, they must find out what money or government benefits people have gotten by lying about military awards. Within one hundred eighty days after this law is passed, the Attorney General must give Congress a report about what they found in their study. The report must also include ideas for how to fix the problems they discovered.
